content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 30, 1989


Thomas M. Hagler
Heller, Ehrman, White and McAuliffe
333 Bush Street
San Francisco, CA 94104-2878

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-384

Dear Mr. Hagler:

In October of last year, you wrote us requesting advice on
behalf of the Sierra Club Committee on Political Education. I have
made a number of attempts to contact you by phone in order to ask
some questions, but have thus far not received any response.
Accordingly, it is my intention to close our file on this matter.

If you still wish to seek advice or if you have any
questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: John G. McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JGM:ld


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

October 4, 1988

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Attention:  Technical Assistance

                    Slate Mailer Organization Rules
                    Request for Clarification

Dear Sir or Madam:

     I am the treasurer of a general purpose recipient
committee, the Sierra Club Committee on Political Education - S.F.
Bay Chapter (FPPC #871601) ("SSCOPE").  SSCOPE is sponsored by the
Sierra Club - S.F. Bay Chapter (the "Sponsor").

     I am writing to request formal written advice from your
office about the application of the new slate mailer organization
rules to a number of different situations.  I have spoken with a
number of people in your office about these questions (including,
most recently, Karla Wardlow), and we concluded that a formal
request for written advice is appropriate.

     The facts and questions are as follows:

     1.  The Sponsor publishes a monthly newsletter
          containing articles of interest to its members.
          This newsletter is mailed to all members, and a
          small number of additional copies are mailed to
          libraries, nonprofit organizations, etc.  The
          newsletter is produced and mailed using the
          sponsor's general funds.  Immediately preceding an
          election, the newsletter contains a list of the
          Sponsor's endorsements of candidates and ballot
          measures.

          Question:  Is the Sponsor a "slate mailer
          organization" subject to registration and reporting
          requirements?

     2.  The Sponsor occasionally publishes at its expense
          additional copies of its endorsement issue and
          distributes these by mail and otherwise to the

Fair Political Practices Commission
October 4, 1988                                Page 2


                    general public. The expense of these additional
                    copies would presumably be a campaign "expenditure"
                    under FPPC Regulation §18225(b)(4)(C).

                    Question: Is the Sponsor a "slate mailer
                    organization" because of its production and
                    distribution of these additional copies?

         3.         SCCOPE produces and mails a slate card for the
                    election to the general public. SCCOPE pays for the
                    expenses of the mailer and reports the expenses on
                    its campaign statements.

                    Question: Is SCCOPE a "slate mailer organization"
                    subject to registration and reporting requirements?

         4.         SCCOPE, three candidates, and another general
                    purpose recipient committee jointly fund the
                    production and distribution of a slate mailer to the
                    general public. No special organization is
                    established to generate the mailer.

                    Question: Which organization, if any, should
                    register and report the activity under the slate
                    mailer rules?

              If you need additional information about the facts or
         wish to discuss this issue further, please call me at (415) 772-
         6490.

                                             Very truly yours,

                                             [illegible]

                                             Thomas M. Hagler
                                             Treasurer

cc:   Ms. Gayle Eads
      Mr. Barry Epstein
      Mr. Brian Woodworth

October 11, 1988


Thomas M. Hagler
Heller, Ehrman, White & McAuliffe
333 Bush Street
San Francisco, CA 94104-2878

Re: 88-384

Dear Mr. Hagler:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on October 5, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
